title: citrus × aurantium subf. omikanto
intro: Citrus × aurantium subf. omikanto, also known as Bitter Orange or Sour Orange, is a subfamilial classification of the citrus aurantium plant that is native to Southeast Asia and has been widely cultivated in various parts of the world for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant is valued for its bitter principle, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation. The medicinal properties of Citrus × aurantium subf. omikanto are attributed to its high concentration of limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Citrus × aurantium subf. omikanto can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote digestion, boost immunity, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
